---
title: "main_analysis.rmd"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-04"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

## Day 1

#Team names: Abril, Kevin, Kylize

## Day 2

#imported HINTSData into R

rel.data <- HINTSData_2020_clean[, c(5,17,16,14,3,6,15,12)] #created subset of relevant data only

## Day 3 Descriptive Analysis & Function Writing

# B Descriptive Analysis

## 1. Statistical Summaries

#Age
 
# Avg Drinks Per Week
 
# Weekly Minutes Moderate Exercise
 
# BMI
 
 
##2. Descriptive Packages in R


##3 Writing Functions
my_summary_func <- 


## Day 4 Univariate Visualization

1.  Distribution of Quality Care

    ![](http://127.0.0.1:22946/graphics/d8b84daf-8e43-4004-b1ec-5c6114b0f0ce.png)

    Distribution of RaceEthn5

    ![](http://127.0.0.1:22946/graphics/2e72fa78-1d65-46bf-8c75-45855a8580bf.png)

2.  Distribution of Age

    ![](http://127.0.0.1:22946/graphics/29026c8e-6025-48e2-a403-8d69ac347782.png)

    Distribution of BMI

    ![](http://127.0.0.1:22946/graphics/c6a0d053-95e7-462c-8056-88ea449ee866.png)

3.  Distribution of Quality Care (ggplot2)

    ![](http://127.0.0.1:22946/graphics/4fce6343-cca7-4fb8-8f3a-dfdd1f256fbd.png)

    Distribution of RaceEthn5 (ggplot2)

    ![](http://127.0.0.1:22946/graphics/70bed6b1-3756-4c0b-9dee-a63ac933dc2f.png)

4.  Distribution of Age (ggplot2)

    ![](http://127.0.0.1:22946/graphics/4617c679-fbbb-4c75-9dbe-a871b2cb6d28.png)

    Distribution of BMI (ggplot2\_

![](http://127.0.0.1:22946/graphics/87409dbe-329f-4531-bafe-55f4facfc7cd.png)

5.  

```         
my_visual_func_I(HINTSData_2020_clean)
```

```         
> my_visual_func_II(HINTSData_2020_clean)
```
#runs summary of variables in the data set
summary(rel.data) 

#stores results in a new name
summary.data <- summary(rel.data) 

#loads current packages 
(.packages()) 

#converts V5 to numerics 
rel.data$V5 <- as.numeric(as.character(rel.data$V5)) 

#structures our previous data 
str(rel.data) 

#see existing values
unique(rel.data$V5) 

#calculates mean on V5
mean(rel.data$V5, na.rm = TRUE) 

#calculates sd of V5
sd(rel.data$V5, na.rm = TRUE) 

#calculates variance of V5
var(rel.data$V5, na.rm = TRUE) 

#downloads the dplyr package
install.packages("dplyr") 

#loads the dyplr package 
library(dplyr) 

#prints our team dataset
rel.data 

#separates smoking status from dataset
mutate(V5 = as.numeric(as.character(V5))) %>% #creates variables for V5 numeric conversion
group_by(SmokingStatus) %>% 

#creates summary stats that give the same output as B1
summarise(mean_V5 = mean(V5, na.rm = TRUE),
          sd_V5   = sd(V5, na.rm = TRUE),
          var_V5  = var(V5, na.rm = TRUE),
          n       = sum(!is.na(V5)))

#installs the skimr package
install.packages("skimr") 

#loads skimr package
library("skimr") 

#outputs a statistical summary of data set 
skim(rel.data) 

#outputs grouped summary
my_summary_func
